threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'quiet pub' exists here—only to discover the real charm is in places blasting drum and bass at 2 AM.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham; an argument broke out over the last pack of Jaffa Cakes. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in flip-flops haggling with a vendor over a watermelon in front of the old synagogue. That’s Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a party happening in a former factory while a bus driver casually serves cocktails. It’s a blend of history and hedonism.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid 16th and Mission after 9 PM—it's like a real-life game of Frogger out here with those buses.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus yesterday with a guy passionately arguing with a pigeon. The pigeon seemed pretty unfazed—classic San Francisco weirdness.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí. They miss the little food stalls in the plazas selling homemade tarta de Santiago, a true local gem.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They stand at the bakery window, mesmerized by the choices, while locals grab their pan a les 8 with a knowing nod.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' without a hint of irony, and you think it's a good idea to wait for a bus in the rain.
Saw a guy on the 36 last night passionately arguing with his reflection in the window. The bus was packed, but he seemed to think he was solo.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a cat lounging atop a stack of old vinyl records outside a Späti, completely unbothered by the chaos around it.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how in Friedrichshain, even the street art seems to have its own local gossip? Walls talking about who’s gone and who's new, like a neighborhood diary.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they instinctively dodge the BART escalator crowd like it’s Pac-Man. Tourists just stand there clueless.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy in a gorilla suit try to board a packed Muni during rush hour. The whole bus erupted in laughter. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss the authentic bars where old locals gather for vermut and jocs de taula.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look at the metro map like it's a treasure map, puzzled at every stop instead of just riding the line.
